one_line_thesis: Lowe's: scaled home improvement compounder; FY2025 FCF ~$13.66/sh; base synthesis 11.8% at ~$214 → watch.

deep_dive.executive_summary:
Lowe's is a scaled home improvement compounder: $86.3 billion of FY2025 sales, $7.7 billion of free cash flow, and a Total Home strategy focused on Pro customers, online, and installation. FY2025 comparable sales were +0.2%; Q1 FY2026 comps were +0.6% on $23.1 billion of net sales. Gross margin compressed in Q1 FY2026 to 32.7% from 33.4% prior year.

At about $214 per share (Yahoo Finance, 2026-07-10), the Lawrence base case implies about 11.8% per year on normalized owner cash of $13.66 per share, below our 15% accumulate hurdle. Durable competitive advantage (Munger moat) is stable: duopo...

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

valuation.interpretation: value_per_share is withheld while any material additive component is unpriced; priced_components_per_share is not a complete security value.

scenario_contract.rule: Cases must change cited causal operating, capital, probability, timing, or financing drivers—not only a terminal multiple.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: A complete security value is intentionally withheld while material components are unpriced. Attribution restarts after the first proof-complete baseline.

total_return_panel.error: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.
total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

coverage.note: Vendor events are discovery evidence; primary-source completeness has not been established.

timeline.label: Q1 FY2026: net sales $23.1B (+10.3% YoY); comparable sales +0.6%; gross margin compressed to 32.7%
FY2025: FBM and ADG acquisitions drive Other segment to $2.2B sales; share repurchases paused to $211M; long-term debt rose to $37.5B
FY2024: net sales $83.7B (-3.0% YoY); comparable sales -0.5%; repurchased $4.1B of stock
Updated Total Home strategy aligned to home improvement demand drivers; Pro customer remains core focus
FY2023: net sales $86.4B (-10.9% YoY post-COVID normalization); comparable sales -3.5%
FY2022: net sales $97.1B peak; comparable sales +0.3%; pandemic-era DIY demand elevated
FY2021: net sales $96.3B (+33.6% YoY); pandemic DIY boom; repurchased $14.1B of stock
Total Home strategy launched: Pro customer, online acceleration, installation services, localization
Marvin R. Ellison became President and CEO (from J.C. Penney); led turnaround and Pro focus
Lowe's went public on NYSE; founded 1921 in North Wilkesboro, NC

industry.structure: Highly fragmented U.S. home improvement market dominated by a duopoly: Lowe's (~1,759 stores) and Home Depot compete on location, assortment, Pro services, and fulfillment. Wholesale distribution (FBM, ADG) adds a B2B layer.
industry.share_shift: Lowe's gained Pro and online share under Total Home but trailed Home Depot on comps in recent years; FY2025 acquisitions of FBM and ADG expand wholesale/building-products reach.
industry.trend: Post-rate-hike housing slowdown pressured transaction counts (-2.8% FY2025) while average ticket rose (+3.0%) on inflation and mix; Pro and installation services are the strategic growth vectors.

risk_claims.claim: Rare negative YoY quarter(s): YoY growth moved to +2.4% to -1.0% YoY (baseline median +8.3% YoY) for two consecutive quarters. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.

*Primary risk: Housing-related volume weakness persists, compressing comparable transactions and operating leverage faster than ticket inflation can offset. [Fact] FY2025 comp transactions were -2.8%; Q1 FY2026 improved to -0.9% but gross margin fell 70 basis points.
Integration/leverage: FBM/ADG acquisitions added debt; failure to integrate or delever delays buyback restart (10-K FY2025).
